claims.evidence_text: Planting window Seed Rate (million/acre) Sept. <1.0 Early-mid Oct. 1.2 – 1.4 After mid-Oct. >1.6
claims.claim_summary: Recommended winter wheat seeding rate increases with later planting: <1.0 million/acre in September, 1.2-1.4 million/acre in early-mid October, >1.6 million/acre after mid-October.

claims.evidence_text: When to start? Soon after hessian fly free date (as a rule of thumb). ... Optimal planting time is critical in setting up a high yield potential.
claims.claim_summary: Optimal winter wheat planting begins soon after the Hessian fly free date; timely planting is critical for high yield potential.

claims.evidence_text: our recent research showed <2% impact of seeding depth on yield. ... Precision planting had lowest depth variability at all sites.
claims.claim_summary: Seeding depth had <2% impact on winter wheat yield; precision planting achieved the lowest seeding depth variability.

claims.evidence_text: yield benefit from use of precision planter and narrow row spacing. ... 8 - 11% yield increase with precision planter (5'' rows) versus drill at 3 of 6 site-years.
claims.claim_summary: Narrow row spacing with a precision planter (5-inch rows) gave 8-11% yield increase versus drill at 3 of 6 site-years in winter wheat.
